title: galium aparine var. pseudoaparine
intro: Galium aparine var. pseudoaparine, also known as sticky wildeed or false cleavers, is a perennial plant in the Rubiaceae family that resembles Galium aparine but lacks the sticky seed pods. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. It is used to treat a variety of health issues, such as arthritis, gout, and skin conditions, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Galium aparine var. pseudoaparine is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used internally or applied topically to the affected area.
